seoTitle: Panasonic Component Solutions | Application Solutions | Authorized Distributor
seoDescription: Panasonic capacitor and relay solutions for industrial power supplies, motor drives, renewable energy, and automotive applications. Technical support and component selection guide available.

faqs.question: What application solutions does Panasonic offer?
faqs.answer: Panasonic provides comprehensive component solutions for diverse applications including industrial power supplies, motor drives, renewable energy systems, automotive electronics, and consumer products. Their solutions cover capacitor applications for filtering, energy storage, and DC bus requirements, as well as relay solutions for power switching and control systems. Panasonic's application engineers work closely with customers to select optimal component series based on voltage, current, switching requirements, and environmental conditions. The company provides technical documentation, application notes, and design support for all major application areas. LiTong's FAE team can assist with component selection, thermal analysis, and lifetime calculations for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss your application requirements and receive tailored component recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select capacitors for switching power supplies?
faqs.answer: Selecting capacitors for switching power supplies requires careful consideration of several parameters. For input filtering, select capacitors with appropriate voltage rating (typically 1.5x the AC line voltage) and adequate ripple current capability. For DC bus applications, calculate required capacitance based on allowable voltage ripple and select capacitors with sufficient ripple current rating and low ESR. For output filtering, consider the switching frequency and select low-ESR capacitors for effective ripple reduction. Panasonic's FR series is ideal for output filtering with its low impedance characteristics. Temperature requirements, lifetime expectations, and physical size constraints must also be considered. Panasonic offers specialized series for each application area.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection tools or contact our FAE team for power supply capacitor recommendations.

faqs.question: What relays are recommended for motor control applications?
faqs.answer: Motor control applications require relays with high switching capacity, good inductive load handling, and long electrical life. For small to medium motors (up to 1HP), Panasonic's ALDP series power relays with AgSnO2 contacts provide reliable switching with 10A capacity. For larger motors or higher current requirements, the DSP series offers up to 16A switching capacity. AgSnO2 contact material is recommended for motor loads due to its excellent resistance to contact welding and material transfer. Always consider the motor's inrush current, which can be 5-10x the running current. Include proper arc suppression such as RC snubbers or varistors to extend contact life. For very frequent switching, consider solid state relays.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor control relay selection and arc suppression recommendations.

faqs.question: Can Panasonic components be used for renewable energy applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Panasonic components are widely used in renewable energy applications including solar inverters, wind turbine power electronics, and energy storage systems. For solar inverters, Panasonic's film capacitors provide excellent DC link and snubber performance with long operational life. The ECQ-P polypropylene series is particularly well-suited for DC link applications with its low losses and high reliability. For power supply sections, the FS series electrolytic capacitors with extended lifetime (10000 hours at 105C) ensure long service life. Panasonic's relays are used for grid connection switching and protection circuits. The company's quality systems ensure consistent performance over the 15-25 year expected life of renewable energy installations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our renewable energy solution or contact our FAE team for component recommendations.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for Panasonic solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Panasonic component solutions including technical consultation, component selection assistance, thermal analysis, lifetime calculations, and application troubleshooting. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Panasonic products across various applications. We offer sample support for evaluation, datasheet and application note access, and design review services. For high-volume customers, we provide demand forecasting, inventory management, and scheduling agreements. Our quality team ensures proper handling and storage of all Panasonic components. Technical training on Panasonic products and applications is available for customer engineering teams.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales or FAE team to discuss your project requirements and available support services.

coreAdvantages.title: Low Impedance Capacitors
coreAdvantages.description: Panasonic's FR series capacitors provide industry-leading low ESR and high ripple current capability, reducing output voltage ripple and improving power supply efficiency. The low impedance minimizes self-heating and extends capacitor life.

coreAdvantages.title: Extended Lifetime Options
coreAdvantages.description: FS series offers 10000 hours at 105C, providing 2x longer life than standard series. This translates to 20+ years of service life in typical industrial environments with proper derating.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Voltage Coverage
coreAdvantages.description: From 16V output filtering to 500V DC bus applications, Panasonic provides capacitors for every stage of industrial power supplies with appropriate safety margins.

coreAdvantages.title: Reliable Power Switching
coreAdvantages.description: ALDP and DSP series relays offer high switching capacity with AgSnO2 contacts optimized for inductive loads. Long electrical life reduces maintenance requirements.

coreAdvantages.title: Expert FAE Support

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a reliable component solution for a new PLC power supply series operating in harsh factory environments with high ambient temperatures up to 60C. The application required long service life and high reliability to minimize maintenance.
customerCases.solution: We recommended Panasonic FS series capacitors for the output stage, providing 10000-hour lifetime at 105C. For EMI filtering, ECQ-E film capacitors were selected for their self-healing properties. ALDP relays were chosen for power control with AgSnO2 contacts for inductive load handling.
customerCases.results: The PLC power supply achieved over 15 years calculated lifetime at operating conditions. Field reliability exceeded 99.5% over 3 years of deployment. The customer reported zero component-related failures across 5000+ units shipped.
customerCases.quantifiedResults: 99.5% reliability over 3 years, zero failures across 5000+ units

faeInsights.insight: Industrial power supply component selection requires balancing multiple factors: ripple current capability, lifetime requirements, physical size, and cost. In my experience, the most common mistake is undersizing capacitors for ripple current, leading to premature failures. I always recommend calculating actual ripple current under worst-case conditions and selecting capacitors with at least 20% margin. For high-reliability applications, consider the FS series despite the higher cost - the extended lifetime often pays for itself through reduced maintenance. Temperature is the enemy of capacitor life - every 10C reduction doubles lifetime. Invest in good thermal design with adequate airflow.
faeInsights.logic: The decision framework for industrial power supply components starts with identifying the application type and operating conditions. For output filtering, prioritize low ESR for ripple reduction. For control circuits, ensure adequate voltage ratings with proper derating. Always apply voltage derating (minimum 20%) and temperature derating when calculating expected lifetime.
keyTakeaways: Always calculate actual ripple current and select capacitors with 20% margin
Temperature is the primary factor affecting component lifetime
Consider FS series for high-reliability applications despite higher initial cost
Use film capacitors for EMI filtering and snubber applications
Implement voltage derating of at least 20% for extended lifetime
commonPitfalls: Undersizing capacitors for ripple current leads to overheating and early failure
Ignoring temperature rise from self-heating in ripple current calculations
Selecting capacitors based only on capacitance value without considering ESR
Inadequate thermal design with insufficient airflow around components
bestPractices: Perform thermal analysis including self-heating from ripple current
Use capacitors with higher temperature ratings than the ambient environment
Implement voltage derating of 20-30% for reliability-critical applications
Provide adequate clearance around components for airflow and heat dissipation
Consider parallel configurations for high-ripple applications

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of capacitors in industrial power supplies?
faqs.answer: Capacitor lifetime in industrial power supplies depends on operating conditions. At 50C ambient with proper voltage derating, Panasonic FR series typically achieves 15-20 years, while FS series can exceed 25 years. The key factors are operating temperature, voltage stress, and ripple current. For every 10C temperature reduction, lifetime approximately doubles. Similarly, operating at 80% of rated voltage instead of 100% can double lifetime. Well-designed power supplies with adequate cooling and proper derating achieve the upper end of these ranges. We recommend calculating lifetime for your specific operating conditions using Panasonic's lifetime models.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations based on your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: How do I select the right relay for power control?
faqs.answer: Selecting relays for power control requires evaluating load characteristics and switching requirements. First, determine the load type (resistive, inductive, or capacitive) and calculate the switching current. For inductive loads like motors, consider inrush currents which can be 5-10x running current. Select contact material based on load type - AgSnO2 is recommended for inductive loads due to its anti-welding properties. Choose coil voltage to match your control system (5V, 12V, 24V DC are common). Ensure the relay's electrical life rating meets your switching frequency requirements. For high-reliability applications, consider relays with higher current ratings than strictly necessary.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your load characteristics for personalized relay recommendations.

customerCases.challenge: The customer experienced relay contact welding issues in their fan motor control systems. Standard relays were failing prematurely due to motor inrush currents and inductive kickback.
customerCases.solution: We recommended upgrading to Panasonic ALDP112 relays with AgSnO2 contacts, which provide superior resistance to welding. RC snubber circuits using ECQ-E film capacitors were added across relay contacts to suppress voltage transients.
customerCases.results: Contact welding issues were completely eliminated. Relay life increased from 50,000 to over 200,000 operations. The customer reduced field service calls by 80% and extended product warranty period.
customerCases.quantifiedResults: 4x increase in relay life, 80% reduction in service calls

faeInsights.insight: Motor control relay selection is critical for reliable operation. The key challenge is handling inductive loads - motors present both high inrush current and inductive kickback when switched off. I always recommend AgSnO2 contact material for motor applications - the anti-welding properties are essential. Don't forget about arc suppression - even with good contacts, inductive kickback will eventually erode contacts without proper snubbing. I typically use RC snubbers with film capacitors across relay contacts. For motor inrush, size relays for at least 2x the running current. And remember that motor load is much harder on contacts than resistive load - a 10A relay might only handle 5A of motor load reliably.
faeInsights.logic: The motor control component selection process begins with understanding the motor characteristics - running current, inrush current, and voltage. Select relays with AgSnO2 contacts rated for at least 2x running current. Implement RC snubber circuits to protect contacts from inductive kickback. Use capacitors rated for the voltage transients expected in the application.
keyTakeaways: Use AgSnO2 contact relays for all motor switching applications
Size relays for 2x motor running current to handle inrush
Implement RC snubber circuits across relay contacts
Film capacitors are ideal for snubber applications
Motor loads are harder on contacts than resistive loads
commonPitfalls: Using standard silver contacts for motor loads leads to welding
Undersizing relays for motor inrush current
Omitting snubber circuits causes contact erosion
Ignoring inductive kickback in relay selection
bestPractices: Always use AgSnO2 or equivalent contact material for motors
Include RC snubber circuits for relays switching inductive loads
Size relays conservatively for motor applications
Consider solid state relays for very frequent switching
Test relay operation under actual load conditions

faqs.question: Why do relays fail when switching motor loads?
faqs.answer: Relay failures in motor applications are typically caused by contact welding or erosion. Motor inrush current (5-10x running current) can weld contacts together, especially with standard silver contacts. Inductive kickback when switching off creates arcing that erodes contact material over time. Solutions include: using AgSnO2 contact material which resists welding, sizing relays for inrush current not just running current, and implementing snubber circuits to suppress voltage transients. Panasonic's ALDP and DSP series with AgSnO2 contacts are specifically designed to handle these challenges.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use AgSnO2 contact relays and implement snubber circuits for reliable motor switching.

faqs.question: What is an RC snubber and why is it needed?
faqs.answer: An RC snubber is a series resistor-capacitor circuit connected across relay contacts or motor terminals. When contacts open, the inductance of the motor winding tries to maintain current flow, creating a high voltage spike (inductive kickback). The snubber provides a path for this current, limiting the voltage spike and reducing arcing across the contacts. Typical values are 0.01-0.1uF capacitor (rated for voltage) with 10-100 ohm resistor. The capacitor absorbs the energy while the resistor limits discharge current. Snubbers can extend relay contact life by 5-10x in motor applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement RC snubbers for all relays switching inductive loads to extend contact life.
